006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

010 *

012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau;
018
019import static java.util.stream.Collectors.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
021import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;
022
023import java.io.*;
024import java.net.*;
025import java.util.*;
026import java.util.stream.*;
027
028import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
029import org.junit.jupiter.api.*;
030
031/**
032 * Shared, marshall-free test utilities common to Juneau test modules.
033 *
034 * <p>
035 * Provides the stable pure-JDK/commons subset of the per-module {@code TestUtils} classes: exception-message
036 * assertions, equality helpers, stream/reader factories, locale/timezone toggles, and URL construction.  Module-level
037 * {@code TestUtils} classes extend this base and add their own marshall-bound helpers (e.g. {@code assertJson},
038 * {@code json}, {@code validateXml}).
039 *
040 * <p>
041 * This class lives in the {@code juneau-test-utils} module, which depends only on {@code juneau-commons} and JUnit, so
042 * it never introduces a {@code juneau-marshall} dependency onto its consumers.
043 */
044public abstract class BasicTestUtils extends Shorts {
045
046   private static final ThreadLocal<TimeZone> SYSTEM_TIME_ZONE = new ThreadLocal<>();
047
048   /** Holds the previous default locale while a test overrides it via {@link #setLocale(Locale)}. */
049   public static final ThreadLocal<Locale> SYSTEM_LOCALE = new ThreadLocal<>();
050
051   /**
052    * Asserts that all the specified values are equal to the first value.
053    *
054    * @param values The values to compare.  Element 0 is the expected value.
055    */
056   public static void assertEqualsAll(Object...values) {
057      for (var i = 1; i < values.length; i++) {
058         assertEquals(values[0], values[i], fs("Elements at index {0} and {1} did not match. {0}={2}, {1}={3}", 0, i, r(values[0]), r(values[i])));
059      }
060   }
061
062   /**
063    * Asserts that the specified actual value does not equal any of the specified values.
064    *
065    * @param actual The actual value.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
066    * @param values The values that must not match the actual value.
067    */
068   public static void assertNotEqualsAny(Object actual, Object...values) {
069      assertNotNull(actual, "Value was null.");
070      for (var i = 0; i < values.length; i++) {
071         assertNotEquals(values[i], actual, fs("Element at index {0} unexpectedly matched.  expected={1}, actual={2}", i, values[i], s(actual)));
072      }
073   }
074
075   /**
076    * Asserts that the specified executable throws an exception whose chained messages contain all the specified substrings.
077    *
078    * @param <T> The expected exception type.
079    * @param expectedType The expected exception type.
080    * @param expectedSubstrings The substrings expected in the exception message chain.
081    * @param executable The code expected to throw.
082    * @return The thrown exception.
083    */
084   public static <T extends Throwable> T assertThrowsWithMessage(Class<T> expectedType, List<String> expectedSubstrings, org.junit.jupiter.api.function.Executable executable) {
085      var exception = Assertions.assertThrows(expectedType, executable);
086      var messages = getMessages(exception);
087      expectedSubstrings.forEach(x -> assertTrue(messages.contains(x), fs("Expected message to contain: {0}.\nActual:\n{1}", x, messages)));
088      return exception;
089   }
090
091   /**
092    * Asserts that the specified executable throws an exception whose chained messages contain the specified substring.
093    *
094    * @param <T> The expected exception type.
095    * @param expectedType The expected exception type.
096    * @param expectedSubstring The substring expected in the exception message chain.
097    * @param executable The code expected to throw.
098    * @return The thrown exception.
099    */
100   public static <T extends Throwable> T assertThrowsWithMessage(Class<T> expectedType, String expectedSubstring, org.junit.jupiter.api.function.Executable executable) {
101      var exception = Assertions.assertThrows(expectedType, executable);
102      var messages = getMessages(exception);
103      assertTrue(messages.contains(expectedSubstring), fs("Expected message to contain: {0}.\nActual:\n{1}", expectedSubstring, messages));
104      return exception;
105   }
106
107   /**
108    * Collects the newline-joined message chain of the specified throwable and all its causes.
109    *
110    * @param t The throwable.
111    * @return The joined message chain.
112    */
113   protected static String getMessages(Throwable t) {
114      return Stream.iterate(t, Throwable::getCause).takeWhile(e -> e != null).map(Throwable::getMessage).collect(joining("\n"));
115   }
116
117   /**
118    * Creates an input stream from the specified string.
119    *
120    * @param in The contents of the stream.
121    * @return A new input stream.
122    */
123   public static final ByteArrayInputStream inputStream(String in) {
124      return new ByteArrayInputStream(in.getBytes());
125   }
126
127   /**
128    * Creates a reader from the specified string.
129    *
130    * @param in The contents of the reader.
131    * @return A new reader.
132    */
133   public static final StringReader reader(String in) {
134      return new StringReader(in);
135   }
136
137   /**
138    * Temporarily sets the default system locale to the specified locale.
139    *
140    * @param v The new default locale.
141    */
142   public static final void setLocale(Locale v) {
143      SYSTEM_LOCALE.set(Locale.getDefault());
144      Locale.setDefault(v);
145   }
146
147   /**
148    * Temporarily sets the default system timezone to the specified timezone ID.
149    *
150    * @param v The new default timezone ID.
151    */
152   public static final synchronized void setTimeZone(String v) {
153      SYSTEM_TIME_ZONE.set(TimeZone.getDefault());
154      TimeZone.setDefault(TimeZone.getTimeZone(v));
155   }
156
157   /**
158    * Restores the default system locale saved by {@link #setLocale(Locale)}.
159    */
160   public static final void unsetLocale() {
161      Locale.setDefault(SYSTEM_LOCALE.get());
162   }
163
164   /**
165    * Restores the default system timezone saved by {@link #setTimeZone(String)}.
166    */
167   public static final synchronized void unsetTimeZone() {
168      TimeZone.setDefault(SYSTEM_TIME_ZONE.get());
169   }
170
171   /**
172    * Constructs a {@link URL} object from a string.
173    *
174    * @param value The URL string.
175    * @return A new {@link URL} object.
176    */
177   public static URL url(String value) {
178      return safe(()->new URI(value).toURL());
179   }
180
181   /**
182    * Returns an empty array of the specified type.
183    *
184    * @param <T> The component type.
185    * @param t The component type.
186    * @return A new empty array of the specified type.
187    */
188   public static <T> T[] ea(Class<T> t) {
189      return CollectionUtils.array(t, 0);
190   }
191}
